assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all 6 output files for terms: ambig, unclear, vague, missing, undefined, unspecified, assumption, clarif. No matches found in any file. The impact map and task descriptions proceed to plan concrete solutions (tsvector indexes, ts_rank relevance, filters, caching) without ever flagging that the feature description is vague (e.g., 'Search should be faster' lacks latency targets, 'Results should be more relevant' lacks relevance criteria, 'Add filters' lacks filter specifications). Zero ambiguities are identified anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all output files for 'Better UI', 'UI', 'mockup', 'frontend', 'design', 'non-MVP', and 'scope'. The string 'Better UI' does not appear in any output file. The impact map states 'All changes are scoped to the trustify-backend repository' but never mentions the non-MVP 'Better UI' requirement from the feature description, never acknowledges its existence, and never explains why it is excluded. There is no discussion of the need for design mockups or the frontend repository.

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files follow the task-description-template.md format. Each contains the required sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and optional sections like Verification Commands, Reuse Candidates, API Changes, and Dependencies as applicable. Section headers match the template format (## Repository, ## Target Branch, etc.).

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: No output file contains the words 'assumption' or 'clarification' or any labeled assumptions. The tasks make numerous implicit assumptions without documenting them: Task 1 assumes PostgreSQL full-text search with English language config; Task 2 assumes ts_rank scoring normalized to [0,1]; Task 3 assumes specific filter parameters (entity_type, date_from, date_to, severity, license); Task 4 assumes 30-second cache TTL and 5-second query timeout. None of these design decisions are labeled as assumptions pending clarification.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task descriptions contain a '## Repository' section with a single repository name 'trustify-backend'. Task 1 line 5: 'trustify-backend', Task 2 line 5: 'trustify-backend', Task 3 line 5: 'trustify-backend', Task 4 line 5: 'trustify-backend', Task 5 line 5: 'trustify-backend'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files contain all four required sections: Task 1 has '## Target Branch' (line 9), '## Description' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 37), '## Test Requirements' (line 45). Task 2 has them at lines 9, 13, 56, 66. Task 3 has them at lines 9, 13, 72, 83. Task 4 has them at lines 9, 13, 49, 59. Task 5 has them at lines 9, 13, 65, 76.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All Files to Modify paths exist in repo-backend.md: migration/src/lib.rs, modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs, tests/api/search.rs, server/src/main.rs. Reuse Candidates also reference valid manifest paths: common/src/db/query.rs, common/src/model/paginated.rs, entity/src/sbom.rs, entity/src/advisory.rs, entity/src/package.rs, modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs. Files to Create paths follow established patterns from the manifest: migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs follows the existing migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs pattern; modules/search/src/model/mod.rs follows the model/service/endpoints module structure used by fundamental modules (e.g., modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs). No invented or non-existent paths are referenced in Files to Modify.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 Implementation Notes reference: 'migration/src/', 'MigrationTrait', 'manager.get_connection().execute_unprepared()', 'entity/src/sbom.rs', 'entity/src/advisory.rs', 'entity/src/package.rs'. Task 2 references: 'SeaORM's Statement::from_sql_and_values', 'AdvisoryService in modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs'. Task 3 references: 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'Axum's Query<SearchParams> extractor'. Task 4 references: 'server/src/main.rs', 'tower_http::set_header', 'tower_http::compression', 'common/src/db/query.rs'. Task 5 references: 'tests/api/search.rs', 'TestContext, TestApp', 'std::time::Instant'. All tasks include specific file paths and code patterns from the repository structure.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task descriptions have '## Target Branch' followed by '`main`'. Task 1 line 9-10: 'main', Task 2 line 9-10: 'main', Task 3 line 9-10: 'main', Task 4 line 9-10: 'main', Task 5 line 9-10: 'main'.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all output files for 'digest', 'sha256', 'hash', 'comment', 'posted'. The only matches for 'hash' are in task-4-caching-optimization.md referring to ETag hashing for HTTP caching (lines 21, 37) and SQL comments (line 39), which are unrelated to description digest comments. No output file contains 'sha256-md:', 'sha256-adf:', '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:', or any digest marker. No digest comments are present in any output.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all output files for 'convention', 'CONVENTIONS', 'applicab', 'Applies:', 'Per CONVENTIONS'. The only match is in task-2-relevance-ranking.md line 49 which mentions 'conventions on combining service-layer queries' in a general sense, not as a convention-applicability enrichment. No output file contains 'Per CONVENTIONS.md', 'Applies: task modifies', or any prescribed-format applicability rationale. Convention-aware enrichment per convention-applicability-rules.md is entirely absent from all outputs.
